- verify-all: Run the full check + clippy sweep across the workspace AND the WASM plugin (panopt-zellij). Use after non-trivial edits, before declaring work done, or when asked to verify the repo builds cleanly. Captures the dual-target dance (workspace builds for host, panopt-zellij builds only for wasm32-wasip1) so you don't have to remember the right manifest-path/target each time.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.text: This is the key documentation. Lines 102–119 of up.rs explain the routing model. Let me read that block and confirm the plugin already uses a background `run_command` (line 1894).

content.content:
100	/// on a selection.
101	///
102	/// Why `Run "zellij" "action" "pipe" ...` instead of `MessagePlugin`: Zellij's
103	/// KDL parser folds `MessagePlugin`'s metadata fields (`name`, `payload`,
104	/// `cwd`, `launch_new`, ...) into the plugin's user-configuration map. The
105	/// runtime then keys plugin identity on `(URL, configuration)`, so a
106	/// `MessagePlugin` keybind whose configuration has any of those metadata
107	/// keys can never match a layout plugin whose configuration does not - Zellij
108	/// launches a *second* plugin instance instead. `zellij action pipe` goes
109	/// through the running session's IPC bus and reaches the existing plugin
110	/// without that mismatch. The cost is a brief transient pane while the CLI
111	/// runs; `close_on_exit true` closes it the moment the pipe is sent.
112	///
113	/// Why `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"`: with five plugin instances
114	/// (one per mode), an unfiltered pipe broadcasts to all five. Narrowing to
115	/// `mode=todos` delivers the message only to the gatekeeper. The plugin
116	/// also early-returns from `pipe()` on non-Todos panes - belt-and-braces if
117	/// the filter ever fails to match.
118	const NEW_PANE_BIND_FROM: &str = r#"bind "Alt n" { NewPane; }"#;
119	const NEW_PANE_BIND_TO: &str = r#"bind "Alt n" { Run "zellij" "action" "pipe" "--name" "panopt:spawn-blank-pane" "--plugin-configuration" "mode=todos" { close_on_exit true; }; }"#;
120	
121	/// `Alt-/` opens the cockpit's popup search across todos and scratchpads. Bare
122	/// `/` would intercept every search keystroke in vim, less, shells, etc. on
123	/// locked content panes, so the binding wears the Alt modifier to stay clear
124	/// of the inner program's namespace. There is no stock Zellij binding to
125	/// retarget, so this is appended into the locked block by
126	/// [`locked_extra_binds`]. The pipe is delivered to the Todos sidebar plugin,
127	/// which spawns the floating `panopt search` TUI. On sidebar plugin panes
128	/// (Normal mode), `Alt-/` is handled inside the plugin's own `handle_key`
129	/// rather than by Zellij - so the gesture works everywhere in the cockpit.
130	const SEARCH_BIND_TO: &str = r#"bind "Alt /" { Run "zellij" "action" "pipe" "--name" "panopt:open-search" "--plugin-configuration" "mode=todos" { close_on_exit true; }; }"#;
131	
132	/// Zellij's stock close/quit keybinds, rewritten to pipe a request to the
133	/// Todos plugin pane (cockpit gatekeeper) instead of acting immediately.
134	/// The plugin refuses the action when an active item would be lost and
135	/// otherwise force-closes through the plugin API. The `pipe` mechanism is
136	/// the same as Alt-N's rewrite - see [`NEW_PANE_BIND_FROM`] for why it goes
137	/// through `zellij action pipe` rather than `MessagePlugin`, and for the
138	/// `--plugin-configuration "mode=todos"` narrowing.
139	///
140	/// The defaults dump these in pane mode, tab mode, search mode (`x` =
141	/// CloseFocus), and a shared_except locked block (`Ctrl q` = Quit). Each FROM
142	/// here is the exact text the dump emits; [`str::replace`] catches every
143	/// occurrence so all modes funnel through the same pipe.
144	const CLOSE_FOCUS_BIND_FROM: &str = r#"bind "x" { CloseFocus; SwitchToMode "Normal"; }"#;
145	const CLOSE_FOCUS_BIND_TO: &str = r#"bind "x" { Run "zellij" "action" "pipe" "--name" "panopt:close-focus-request" "--plugin-configuration" "mode=todos" { close_on_exit true; }; SwitchToMode "Normal"; }"#;
146	const CLOSE_TAB_BIND_FROM: &str = r#"bind "x" { CloseTab; SwitchToMode "Normal"; }"#;
147	const CLOSE_TAB_BIND_TO: &str = r#"bind "x" { Run "zellij" "action" "pipe" "--name" "panopt:close-tab-request" "--plugin-configuration" "mode=todos" { close_on_exit true; }; SwitchToMode "Normal"; }"#;
148	const QUIT_BIND_FROM: &str = r#"bind "Ctrl q" { Quit; }"#;
149	const QUIT_BIND_TO: &str = r#"bind "Ctrl q" { Run "zellij" "action" "pipe" "--name" "panopt:quit-request" "--plugin-configuration" "mode=todos" { close_on_exit true; }; }"#;

content.text: I now have a complete, verified picture of the mechanism. Let me present the plan.

Here's my implementation plan for **#110 — "make hotkeys that focus sidebar panes"**.

## What I verified

- The five sidebar views (Todos/Agents/Terminals/Commands/Scratchpads) are five **distinct plugin instances** of the same WASM, keyed on config `mode=<m>` (`Mode` enum, `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:62`).
- Each instance captures its own pane id at load: `self.plugin_pane = Some(PaneId::Plugin(get_plugin_ids().plugin_id))` (`main.rs:590`), and already focuses itself elsewhere via `focus_pane_with_id(plugin, false, false)`.
- A plugin **cannot** see its siblings' pane ids — `ingest_panes` skips plugin panes (`main.rs:1101`). So cross-instance focus must be done by *messaging the target instance, which then focuses its own pane*.
- The proven cross-instance mechanism in this repo (documented at `up.rs:102-117`): `zellij action pipe --name <n> --plugin-configuration "mode=<m>"` reaches **only** the matching instance (unfiltered = broadcasts to all five). The plugin already uses background `run_command`-family calls, and `pipe()` runs on every instance.

## Mechanism

`Alt+1..5` → tell target instance to focus itself, via a new `panopt:focus-pane` pipe. Target maps: `1→Todos, 2→Agents, 3→Terminals, 4→Commands, 5→Scratchpads`.

## Changes (all in `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`)

**1. `Mode` helpers (near `letter()`, ~`main.rs:90`)**
- `slug()` → `"todos"|"agents"|...` (reverse of `parse`, for the `mode=` filter).
- `hotkey_hint()` → `"alt+1"|...` (for the title).
- `from_hotkey(c: char)` → `Option<Mode>` (`'1'→Todos`, …).

**2. `handle_key` (insert above the bare `'1'`/`'2'` sort arms, ~`main.rs:1537`)** — Alt-guarded so it never shadows the bare sort keys:
```rust
BareKey::Char(c @ '1'..='5') if key.key_modifiers.contains(&KeyModifier::Alt) => {
    if let Some(target) = Mode::from_hotkey(c) {
        run_command(
            &["zellij", "action", "pipe", "--name", "panopt:focus-pane",
              "--plugin-configuration", &format!("mode={}", target.slug())],
            BTreeMap::new(),
        );
    }
}
```
(Background `run_command` does **not** open a focus-stealing pane, unlike the keybind `Run` path.)

**3. `pipe()` — new arm above the `mode != Todos` guard (~`main.rs:687`)**, alongside `close-search`:
```rust
"panopt:focus-pane" => {
    if let Some(plugin) = self.plugin_pane {
        focus_pane_with_id(plugin, false, false);
    }
    return true;
}
```
Zellij's `mode=` filter already delivers only to the target, so it just focuses its own pane. (Optional belt-and-braces: also send `--payload <slug>` and ignore if it ≠ `self.mode.slug()`, mirroring the repo's defensive style.)

**4. Title hint (`frame_title`, `main.rs:833`)** — prepend the hint to `base` so it survives truncation (counts get dropped first):
```rust
let base = format!("[{}] {}", self.mode.hotkey_hint(), self.mode.label());
```
Yields `[alt+1] Todos [all] (1-36/74)` exactly as the todo asks; non-Todos panes get `[alt+2] Agents (3)` etc.

**5. `help_lines` (`main.rs:901`)** — add an `alt+1..5  focus sidebar pane` line to the shared keys block.

## Optional — also works from content/agent panes (Part B)

When a content pane is focused (auto-Locked), the plugin doesn't get the key. To make `Alt+N` work there too (matching how Alt-/ works everywhere), add 5 binds to `locked_extra_binds()` (`up.rs:673`), each `Run "zellij" "action" "pipe" "--name" "panopt:focus-pane" "--plugin-configuration" "mode=<m>"`. Without Part B, the hotkeys work sidebar→sidebar only.

## Tests / verify

- `up.rs` keybind tests assert exact counts of `"--plugin-configuration" "mode=todos"` (`up.rs:955`, `:1135`) — Part B's Todos bind would bump those; I'll update the counts.
- Add a unit test for `Mode::{slug,from_hotkey,hotkey_hint}` round-trips.
- Build the WASM target (`cargo check --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1`) + workspace via the `verify-all` skill, then `just refresh` to reload the live plugin and smoke-test the hotkeys.

## Open question

Do you want **Part B** (works from content/agent panes too), or just the sidebar→sidebar core (Part A)?

I'd lean toward including Part B — jumping back to a sidebar from a focused agent pane is the most lazygit-like use. Want me to proceed with **A + B**, **A only**, or adjust anything first?

content.text:
That changes the design in a clean way. Here's the key finding and the refined plan.

## The popup problem (and the fix)

When any floating popup is focused (search dialog, delete/close-gate dialogs), the cockpit is in **Locked** mode — same mode as a focused content pane (`ingest_panes` only treats a *plugin* pane as "sidebar focused", `main.rs:1094`). Zellij input mode is global, so a locked-block `Alt N` keybind **can't** distinguish "tiled content pane" from "floating popup" by mode alone — it would fire inside the search popup and yank focus away. That's exactly what you don't want.

The signal that *does* distinguish them: the focused pane's `is_floating` flag, which every instance already sees in the `PaneManifest`. So I'll gate in the receiving plugin, not in the keybind.

## Unified design (A + B share one pipe)

Both the sidebar path and the content-pane path emit the **same** pipe, routed straight to the target instance, which focuses *itself* unless a floating pane is focused:

1. **`Mode` helpers** (`main.rs:~90`): `slug()` (`"todos"…`), `hotkey_hint()` (`"alt+1"…`), `from_hotkey('1')→Todos`.

2. **Track floating focus** (`ingest_panes`, `main.rs:1075`): add a `focused_is_floating: bool` field, updated only from clean (non-transient) manifests exactly like the existing `sidebar_focused` — record whether the focused pane has `is_floating` set.

3. **Pipe handler** (`pipe()`, above the `mode != Todos` guard, `main.rs:687`):
   ```rust
   "panopt:focus-pane" => {
       if !self.focused_is_floating {
           if let Some(plugin) = self.plugin_pane {
               focus_pane_with_id(plugin, false, false);
           }
       }
       return true;
   }
   ```
   Floating popup focused → no-op (your "except popups"). Sidebar or tiled content pane focused → target focuses itself.

4. **Part A — sidebar panes** (`handle_key`, Alt-guarded, before the bare `1`/`2` sort arms): `Alt+1..5` → `run_command(["zellij","action","pipe","--name","panopt:focus-pane","--plugin-configuration", &format!("mode={}", target.slug())])`. (Plugin `run_command` opens no focus-stealing pane.)

5. **Part B — content panes** (`locked_extra_binds`, `up.rs:673`): five binds, `bind "Alt 1" { Run "zellij" "action" "pipe" "--name" "panopt:focus-pane" "--plugin-configuration" "mode=todos" {close_on_exit true;}; }` … through `mode=scratchpads`.

6. **Title hint** (`frame_title`, `main.rs:833`): `let base = format!("[{}] {}", self.mode.hotkey_hint(), self.mode.label());` → `[alt+1] Todos [all] (1-36/74)`.

7. **Help overlay** (`help_lines`, `main.rs:901`): add `alt+1..5  focus sidebar pane`.

8. **Tests**: update the keybind-count assertions in `up.rs` (`:955`, `:1135`) for the new `mode=…` binds; add `Mode::{slug,from_hotkey,hotkey_hint}` round-trip test. Then `verify-all` (workspace + wasm) and `just refresh` to smoke-test live.

**One tradeoff to flag:** the guard keys off "focused pane is floating," so *any* floating pane (not just popups) is excluded — but popups are the only floating panes in practice, so this matches your intent with the cleanest available signal.

Want me to start implementing this (I'll `todo_start #110` first)?

Clippy clean, release plugin `.wasm` + workspace rebuilt, keybind test passes.

The root cause: I'd used `--payload` as a flag, but `zellij action pipe` takes the payload as a **positional arg after `--`** (`--name X -- <payload>`, which broadcasts to all listening plugins). Both call sites and the test now use `--`.

Because the **keybinds (Part B) are baked into the Zellij config at `panopt up` time** — not on a plugin reload — you need a fresh launch for those to take effect. The artifacts are built; relaunch from your terminal:

```
! panopt up
```

Then test:
- From **Todos**: `Alt+2`→Agents, `Alt+3`→Terminals, `Alt+4`→Commands, `Alt+5`→Scratchpads.
- From a **focused agent/content pane**: `Alt+1`→Todos.
- With the **search popup** open: `Alt+N` does nothing.

One thing I want to confirm empirically with this run: Part A (from a focused **sidebar** pane) routes through the plugin's own `run_command "zellij" …`. If the content-pane keybinds (Part B) work but jumping **between sidebar panes** doesn't, that means the plugin can't find `zellij` on its `PATH`, and I'll switch Part A to focus the sibling pane directly by id (no subprocess). Let me know which cases work.
